title: toxicodendron radicans subsp. barkleyi
intro: Toxicodendron radicans subsp. barkleyi, also known as poison oak, is a subspecies of the poison oak plant native to North America. It contains toxic compounds called urushiol oils, which can cause skin irritation, inflammation, and allergic reactions in some individuals. However, in traditional medicine, the plant's roots and leaves have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The roots of the plant can be prepared as a decoction or tincture, while the leaves can be used to make a salve or ointment for topical application.
